title: 第 35 節
en: ' Declare it, Sir,' he replied.

He said to him : ' Fire is one quarter, the sun is one quarter, the moon is one quarter, lightning is one quarter. This is a foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, and called ^yotishmat (full of light).

IV PRAPAFFAKA, 8 KHAiVDA, 3. 6$ J 4. 'He who knows this and meditates on the foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, by the name of Cyotishmat, becomes full of light in this world. He conquers the worlds which are full of light, whoever knows this and meditates on the foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, by the name of 6yotishmat.
Eighth Khanda.
1. 'A diver-bird (Madgu, meant for Pra/^a) will declare to you another foot of Brahman.'

(After these words of the Ha;/zsa), Satyakama, on the morrow, drove the cows onward. And when they came towards the evening, he lighted a fire, penned the cows, laid wood on the fire, and sat down behind the fire, looking toward the east.
2. Then a diver flew near and said to him : 'Satyakama.' He replied: 'Sir.'
3. The diver said : ' Friend, I will declare unto you one foot of Brahman.'
' Declare it, Sir,' he replied.

He said to him : ' Breath is one quarter, the eye is one quarter, the ear is one quarter, the mind is one quarter. This is a foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, and called Ayatanavat (having a home).

' He who knows this and meditates on the foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, by the A name of Ayatanavat, becomes possessed of a home in this world. He conquers the worlds which offer a home, whoever knows this and meditates on the foot of Brahman, consisting of four quarters, by the A name of Ayatanavat.
64 A'tfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
Ninth Khanda.
i. Thus he reached the house of his teacher. The teacher said to him : ' Satyakama.' He replied : ' Sir.'
2. The teacher said : ' Friend, you shine like one who knows Brahman. Who then has taught you1?' He replied: 'Not men. But you only, Sir, I wish, should teach me 2 ;

3. ' For I have heard from men like you, Sir, that only knowledge which is learnt from a teacher (A/tarya), leads to real good.' Then he taught him the same knowledge. Nothing was left out, yea, nothing was left out.
Tenth Khanda3.

1. Upakosala Kamalayana dwelt as a Brahma^arin (religious student) in the house of Satyakama Cabala. He tended his fires for twelve years. But the teacher, though he allowed other pupils (after they had learnt the sacred books) to depart to their own homes, did not allow Upakoi'ala to depart.

2. Then his wife said to him : ' This student, who is quite exhausted (with austerities), has carefully tended your fires. Let not the fires themselves blame you, but teach him.' The teacher, however, went away on a journey without having taught him.
3. The student from sorrow was not able to eat.
1 It would have been a great offence if Satyakama had accepted instruction from any man, except his recognised teacher.
2 The text should be, bhagavaws tv eva me kame bruyat (me kame = mame^Myam).
3 The Upako^ala-vidya teaches first Brahman as the cause, and then in its various forms, and is therefore called atmavidya and agnividya.

IV PRAPA777AKA, II KHAA^DA, I. 65 Then the wife of the teacher said to him : ' Student, eat! Why do you not eat?' He said: 'There are many desires in this man here, which lose themselves in different directions. I am full of sorrows, and shall take no food.'
4. Thereupon the fires said among themselves : ' This student, who is quite exhausted, has carefully tended us. Well, let us teach him.' They said to him :
5. ' Breath is Brahman, Ka (pleasure) is Brahman, Kha (ether) is Brahman.'

He said : ' I understand that breath is Brahman, but I do not understand Ka or Kha V They said : ' What is Ka is Kha, what is Kha is Ka V They therefore taught him Brahman as breath, and as the ether (in the heart)3.
Eleventh Khaaz>a.

1. After that the Garhapatya fire4 taught him: 1 Earth, fire, food, and the sun (these are my forms, or 1 I do not understand, he means, how Ka, which means pleasure, and is non-eternal, and how Kha, which means ether, and is not intelligent, can be Brahman.

2 The commentator explains as follows : — Ka is pleasure, and Kha is ether, but these two words are to determine each other mutually, and thus to form one idea. Ka therefore does not mean ordinary pleasures, but pleasures such as belong to Kha, the ether. And Kha does not signify the ordinary outward ether, but the ether in the heart, which alone is capable of pleasure. What is meant by Ka and Kha is therefore the sentient ether in the heart, and that is Brahman, while Prawa, breath, is Brahman, in so far as it is united with the ether in the heart.
zh: 「請說出來吧，先生，」他回答道。
他對他說：『火是四分之一，太陽是四分之一，月亮是四分之一，閃電是四分之一。這是梵天（Brahman）的一足，由四個四分之一組成，稱為 *jyotismat*（充滿光明）。
IV PRAPAFFAKA, 8 KHANDA, 3. 6-4. 「凡知此者並冥思梵天之足，其足由四分組成，名曰焦蒂什瑪特（Cyotishmat），此人在此世界中充滿光明。凡知此者並冥思梵天之足，其足由四分組成，名曰焦蒂什瑪特者，征服充滿光明之諸世界。」
第八分篇
「一隻潛水鳥（Madgu，意指普拉那 Pra/^a）將向你宣示梵天的另一足。」
（哈里撒的這些言語之後）薩帝亞卡摩在翌日驅趕牛群前行。當牛群在傍晚時分抵達時，他生起火焰、將牛群圈起、在火上堆放木柴，然後坐在火後，面朝東方。
2. 然後一位潛水者飛近他身邊說道：「薩帝耶迦摩。」他回答：「先生。」
3. 潛水者說：「朋友，我將為你闡明梵天（Brahman）的一足。」
「請說出來，先生，」他回答道。
他對他說：「呼吸是四分之一，眼睛是四分之一，耳朵是四分之一，心智是四分之一。這是梵天（Brahman）的一足，由四個四分之一組成，稱為有家者（Ayatanavat）。
誰若知曉此事，且對梵天之足——由四個部分所組成，以阿耶檀那瓦（Ayatanavat）為其名號——冥思默想，便在此世間得有一個家園。誰若知曉此事，且對梵天之足——由四個部分所組成，以阿耶檀那瓦（Ayatanavat）為其名號——冥思默想，便征服那些提供家園的世界。
64 愛坦陀奧義書（AITAREYA-UPANISHAD）。
第九篇章
i. 如是，他來到了他的老師之家。老師對他說：「薩帝亞卡瑪。」他回答：「先生。」
2. 老師說：「朋友，你閃耀著猶如一個知曉梵（Brahman）的人。那麼誰教導過你呢？」他回答：「不是人。但我只願，先生，您應該教導我；
3. 「我從像您這樣的人們那裡聽說，先生，只有從師傅（Acharya）那裡學習的知識才能導向真正的善。」隨後他便教導了他同樣的知識。沒有遺漏任何東西，是的，沒有遺漏任何東西。
第十章節¹
烏帕科薩拉‧卡瑪拉亞那（Upakosala Kamalayana）作為梵行者（宗教學生）住在薩堤亞卡瑪‧卡巴拉（Satyakama Cabala）的家中。他侍奉聖火十二年。但這位教師雖然允許其他弟子（在他們學習了聖典之後）回歸自己的家鄉，卻不允許烏帕科薩拉離去。
2. 其時其妻對他說：『這位學生已因苦行而精疲力竭，卻仍細心照料你的聖火。莫使聖火本身譴責你，反當教導他。』然而，那位教師竟未曾教導他便遠行他鄉。
3. 那位因悲傷而來求學的人無法進食。
倘若薩帝亞卡瑪（Satyakama）接受任何人的教導，除了他所認可的老師之外，那將是一種極大的冒犯。
本文應為 bhagavaws tv eva me kame bruyat（me kame = mame^Myam）。
《奧義書》教導首先是梵天（Brahman）作為原因，其次是其各種形式，因此被稱為自我知識（atmavidya）和火祭知識（agnividya）。
IV PRAPA777AKA, II KHAA^DA, I. 65 爾時，教師之妻謂之曰：「學生，進食吧！汝何以不食？」彼答曰：「此人身中有眾多欲望，四散紛亂。我心滿憂愁，故不進食。」
4. 於是眾火便彼此商議道：『這位學生精疲力竭，卻細心照料了我們。好吧，讓我們來教導他。』他們對他說：
5. 「氣息是梵（Brahman），喜樂（Ka）是梵，虛空（Kha）是梵。」
他說：「我理解呼吸即梵天（Brahman），但我不明白何為迦（Ka）或迦（Kha）。」他們說：「迦即迦，迦即迦。」因此他們將呼吸以及以太（心中之以太）教導給他，作為梵天。
第十一卡紮（Khaaz>a）
在那之後，家祭火（Garhapatya fire）教導他：「地、火、食物與太陽（這些是我的形式），或『我不理解』，他的意思是說，『迦』（Ka）意指快樂，是非永恆的，以及『佉』（Kha）意指乙太，是無智的，這兩者如何能是梵天？」
註釋者的解釋如下：——迦（Ka）是喜樂，空（Kha）是以太，但這兩個字必須相互決定，因而形成一個觀念。迦不是指普通的喜樂，而是屬於空（以太）的喜樂。空也不是指普通的外在以太，而是心中的以太，唯有它才能具有喜樂。迦和空所指的因此是心中的有感知的以太，那就是梵（Brahman），而普拉瓦（Prawa，呼吸）是梵，就其與心中以太相合的程度而言。
